name: Niger-Congo (family node)
notes: STRUCTURAL ANCESTRY NODE (not a modelled G2P target). Niger-Congo (Atlantic-Congo) is the large African phylum whose Volta-Niger branch includes Yoruba and Igbo and whose Bantu branch is rooted separately at Proto-Bantu (bnt). It exists here only so the ancestry / phylogenetic-distance metrics of the modern varieties that reference it have a node to resolve to; NO phonology is claimed or reconstructed and the minimal grapheme set is a placeholder (afa.json / trk.json stub pattern).

sources.author: Williamson, K. & Blench, R.
sources.title: Niger-Congo (in: African Languages: An Introduction)
sources.notes: Reference for Niger-Congo classification (structural node only).
